# Cross-model panel: Vanilla / X+M / SE × \{minimax, gemini\} on CC × test-17

*Generated by research-copilot.*

## Setup

- Harness: Claude Code (default-off env, $\texttt{GEOS\_HOOK\_POSTTOOLUSE}$ unset → autocamp-experiment-state parity)
- Backbones: \texttt{minimax/minimax-m2.7} and \texttt{google/gemini-3-flash-preview} via OpenRouter
- Task set: test-17, $n=1$ seed (cost-driven; gemini I/O pricing is ${\sim}3.6\times$ DSv4-flash on input and ${\sim}11\times$ on output)
- Cells: Vanilla (F0), X+M (F4), SE (v3 self-evolved monolith)
- Same primer files and cheatsheets as autocamp DSv4 runs

## Headline numbers

| cell | minimax fa0 | gemini fa0 | CC × DSv4 (3-seed mean) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Vanilla | $0.821$ (1 fail) | $0.768$ | $0.910 \pm 0.024$ |
| **X+M** | **$0.392$ (8 fails)** | **$0.797$** | $0.921 \pm 0.007$ |
| **SE** | **$0.861$ (0 fails)** | $0.757$ (1 fail) | $0.919 \pm 0.020$ |

Failure counts are out of $17$ tasks (single seed).

## The minimax × X+M pathology

The most striking result: minimax × X+M scores $0.392$ with $8/17$ tasks failing entirely (0 XML files written; agent exits at ${\sim}25$s).

Inspection of one failed task (\texttt{TutorialSneddon}) shows minimax producing **pseudo MCP tool calls** as text — emitting strings like \texttt{<minimax:tool\_call><invoke name="mcp\_\_geos-rag\_\_search\_navigator"><parameter ...>} into the conversation rather than actually invoking tools. The runtime does not parse these as tool calls, so they yield no observable result. The agent receives no data, gets stuck, and gives up.

This failure is gated on **the X+M cell registering the xmllint MCP server but \emph{not} the geos-rag MCP server.} minimax learns from training data that "MCP available" implies geos-rag-style tools, and hallucinates calls to those tools. When the calls land in nothing, the agent's plan fails. The Vanilla cell does not exhibit this failure (no MCP servers are registered, so no hallucinated MCP calls). The SE cell does not exhibit this failure either, because SE registers \emph{both} geos-rag and xmllint MCP servers — the pseudo-calls land on real handlers.

The trajectory metadata makes this concrete: in the failed minimax × X+M tasks, \texttt{per\_tool\_counts} is empty (no real tool invocations) while \texttt{pseudo\_tool\_counts} contains 1--3 calls per task to \texttt{mcp\_\_geos-rag\_\_search\_*} despite the geos-rag server being absent.

## Three findings for the paper

\textbf{(1) Adapter ranking is not backbone-invariant.}
On CC × DSv4-flash: F0 ($0.910$) $<$ F4 ($0.921$) $\approx$ SE ($0.919$). On CC × minimax: F4 ($0.392$) $\ll$ F0 ($0.821$) $\approx$ SE ($0.861$). On CC × gemini: F0 ($0.768$) $<$ F4 ($0.797$); SE ($0.757$) $\approx$ F0. The adapter recommendation we make on DSv4 does not transfer cleanly.

\textbf{(2) The X+M cell has a hidden dependency on RAG MCP availability for minimax.}
The cleanest cross-model panel reveals a backbone-specific failure mode that did not appear in the autocamp DSv4 factorial: registering one MCP server (xmllint) without registering geos-rag triggers minimax's pseudo-tool-call hallucination. SE works on minimax precisely because its plugin includes geos-rag. The reframe for the paper is that "X+M" on the autocamp factorial really means "X+M, with no geos-rag" --- a non-orthogonality the factorial design did not isolate.

\textbf{(3) Vanilla cross-model gap is ${\sim}9$--$14$pp.}
On Vanilla, minimax loses $9$pp vs DSv4 ($0.910\to0.821$); gemini loses $14$pp ($0.910\to0.768$). The harness alone cannot close this gap: the harness-less floor on minimax is $0.333$ and the Vanilla CC harness lifts that to $0.821$ ($+0.488$), but minimax simply doesn't author GEOS XML as fluently as DSv4 does.

## Cross-model variance reduction (vs DSv4 σ)

We have only $n=1$ seed per cross-model cell, so we cannot quantify σ. The sub-task failure counts are a rough proxy:

| cell | DSv4 fails (51) | minimax fails (17) | gemini fails (17) |
|---|---:|---:|---:|
| Vanilla | $0$ | $1$ | $0$ |
| X+M | $0$ | $8$ | $0$ |
| SE | $0$ | $0$ | $1$ |

DSv4 has zero failures across $51$ attempts. Cross-model has 1--8 failures across $17$ attempts. The reliability advantage of DSv4 on this benchmark is real and substantial.

## What this means for the paper's recommendations

The autocamp paper currently recommends X+M as the adapter for "the cell with xmllint and memory cheatsheet on, no RAG, no Stop-hook". After cross-model, that recommendation needs a footnote:

> The X+M recommendation transfers to gemini-3-flash-preview ($+2.9$pp over Vanilla on test-17) but breaks catastrophically on minimax-m2.7 ($-43$pp due to a backbone-specific pseudo-tool-call failure when an MCP server is registered without the model's expected ecosystem of MCP tools). A safer cross-model recommendation is the SE plugin, which on CC reaches $0.861$ on minimax (vs Vanilla's $0.821$) and $0.757$ on gemini (within seed noise of Vanilla), at the cost of higher implementation complexity.

This is a more nuanced story than the original X+M-wins-on-test-17 framing, but it is also a more useful finding: it tells future builders to pair MCP servers carefully with the backbone's tool-call learned distribution.

## Cost notes

We ran 6 cell-runs (3 cells × 2 models) × 17 tasks × 1 seed = $102$ task-runs, total wall time ${\sim}2{:}05$ (08:48 to 10:53 UTC). Cost: not yet tabulated; minimax pricing $0.30/1.20$ (in/out) and gemini pricing $0.50/3.00$ per million tokens make gemini the dominant share.

## Result paths

- Trajectories: \texttt{/data/shared/geophysics\_agent\_data/data/eval/cross\_model\_2026-05-03/<model\_tag>/<cell>/<run>/}
- Per-task scoring: \texttt{/tmp/cm\_<model>\_<cell>\_results/} (regenerable from trajectories)
- Summary JSON: \texttt{/tmp/cm\_<model>\_<cell>.json}
